full_response:
Let  

• Σ be the alphabet,  |Σ| = σ  (> 2).  
• P[0 … m–1] the pattern Balbo wants to find (all characters are readable).  
• R[0 … n–1] the inscription written on the 2nd ring; some symbols are the special
  “?” (unreadable / wildcard) that matches every character in Σ.  
• The ring is cyclic, so a length-m factor can start at any offset
  i∈{0,…,n–1} and continues modulo n.

We must output all offsets i such that for every j = 0 … m–1  

  R[(i+j) mod n] = P[j]  or  R[(i+j) mod n] = “?”.

Target running time:  O(σ·(n+m) log (n+m)).



-------------------------------------------------
1.  Linearising the ring
-------------------------------------------------
Let L = n+m–1.  
Form a linear string S[0 … L–1] by copying the ring far enough:

  S[k] = R[ k mod n ]  for k = 0 … L–1 .

Every cyclic length-m factor of the ring appears exactly once in S
starting at positions 0 … n–1.



-------------------------------------------------
2.  Character–wise indicator vectors
-------------------------------------------------
For every character c ∈ Σ build two {0,1}-vectors.

Text vector (length L)                 Pattern vector (length m)
-----------------------------------------------------------------
T_c[k] = 1 iff S[k] = c                P_c[m–1–j] = 1 iff P[j] = c
           and S[k] ≠ “?”                            (reverse order)
else 0                                            else 0

Reversing P_c lets ordinary convolution align the pattern left-to-right.



-------------------------------------------------
3.  Computing exact matches of individual characters
-------------------------------------------------
Convolve each pair (T_c, P_c) with an FFT:

  R_c = T_c * P_c              // length ≤ 2L

For every offset i = 0 … n–1

  R_c[i + m – 1]   = | { j |  0≤j<m, P[j]=c,  S[i+j]=c } |.



-------------------------------------------------
4.  Handling wildcards “?”
-------------------------------------------------
Create U[k] = 1 iff S[k] = “?” , else 0, and its prefix sum
  pref[–1]=0,  pref[k]=pref[k–1]+U[k].

Unreadable symbols inside the window that starts at i:

  Q[i] = pref[i+m–1] – pref[i–1]     (0 ≤ i ≤ n–1).



-------------------------------------------------
5.  Putting the pieces together
-------------------------------------------------
For each offset i = 0 … n–1

   totalMatched(i) = Q[i]                               // “?”’s
                    + ∑_{c∈Σ}  R_c[i + m – 1].          // exact matches

P matches at i  ⇔  totalMatched(i) = m.

Return all such i (or the fact that at least one exists).



-------------------------------------------------
6.  Correctness proof
-------------------------------------------------
Claim  The algorithm reports exactly the cyclic positions where P
occurs under the “?” wildcard interpretation.

Proof.  
Fix an offset i.

• For every j with S[i+j] = “?” the character P[j] is matched by the
 wild-card; those matches are counted in Q[i].

• For every readable character c the position j contributes 1 to
 R_c[i+m–1]  iff  S[i+j]=c=P[j].  
 Therefore ∑_c R_c[i+m–1] counts all pattern positions matched by an
 identical readable character.

Hence

  totalMatched(i) =
  (# positions matched by “?”) + (# positions matched exactly).

If totalMatched(i)=m all m pattern positions are matched, so P occurs
starting at i.  
Conversely, if P occurs at i then every pattern position is either a
“?” or an equal character, so totalMatched(i)=m. ∎



-------------------------------------------------
7.  Running-time analysis
-------------------------------------------------
Length of every vector ≤ Θ(n+m) = Θ(L).  
Each FFT convolution costs Θ(L log L).

We perform one convolution per alphabet character: σ in total.

Time    T(n,m) = σ·Θ(L log L) = Θ(σ (n+m) log(n+m)).

Space   O(σ L) for the indicator vectors plus O(L) for the FFT scratch
         memory –  overall O(σ (n+m)).



-------------------------------------------------
8.  Pseudocode
-------------------------------------------------
MatchRing(P[0…m-1], R[0…n-1], Σ)  
    L ← n + m – 1  
    S[0…L-1] ← R[k mod n]  
    for c in Σ do  
        build T_c[0…L-1]    // 1 iff S[k]=c, readable  
        build P_c[0…m-1]    // reversed  
        R_c ← FFT-Convolve(T_c, P_c)  
    build prefix sum pref of U[k]=1 iff S[k]=“?”  
    for i = 0 … n–1 do  
        Q ← pref[i+m-1] – pref[i-1]  
        matches ← Q  
        for c in Σ do matches ← matches + R_c[i+m-1]  
        if matches = m report i



\boxed{An occurrence of the length\!-\!m word on the second ring can be
found (and all occurrences listed) in\;O(|\Sigma|\,(n+m)\log(n+m))\;time
by computing, via FFT convolutions, for every character c\in\Sigma the
number of exact c–matches in each cyclic window, adding the number of
wild-cards, and accepting the offsets where the total equals m.}

Confidence: 0.46
